title: Day 12: Day 12 — Compensation Structures
content:
# Day 12 — Compensation Structures

## Topic
How to present compensation in a way that helps agents make a clear, numbers-based decision.

## 3–5 Key Points
- **Model fit beats headline split:** 100% commission, caps, desk-fee, and traditional split models each fit different production levels and business styles.
- **Focus on net, not hype:** Agents care about what they actually keep after brokerage fees, lead costs, marketing, and admin support.
- **Show realistic earnings scenarios:** Walk through 2–3 concrete examples (new agent, mid producer, top producer) so compensation feels real, not theoretical.
- **Incentives should drive behavior:** Bonuses and perks work best when tied to retention, production consistency, and culture contribution.
- **Transparency builds trust:** Clear fee breakdowns and no-surprise economics reduce friction and shorten decision time.

## Why It Matters
Comp plans are emotional and financial. If you explain them poorly, you lose great agents even when your offer is objectively better. If you explain them clearly with real math, you position your brokerage as credible, agent-first, and worth switching for.
